text.title: RSpec Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Agent failure modes in RSpec suites: stubbing the SUT, let shadowing, before(:all) confusion, and mock-heavy specs.

antipattern.description: Stubbing the method under test: agent stubs the very service method being spec'd — spec always passes regardless of the implementation.
antipattern.reason: Stubbing the SUT detaches the test from the real code path. Force prompts to explicitly name what is mocked vs real before generating the spec.

antipattern.description: let shadowing: inner context redefines let(:user) with different attributes; outer spec examples silently use the wrong user.
antipattern.reason: RSpec resolves let from the nearest enclosing scope, not the outermost. Lint via rubocop-rspec ContextLeakCheck to catch redefined lets.

antipattern.description: before(:all) for DB setup: seed records created before the suite but within a transactional spec — transaction rolls back the seed, second run finds nothing.
antipattern.reason: before(:all) runs outside the per-example transaction. Use before(:each) or let! for records that must exist per example. Use shared_context for reusable setup.

antipattern.description: build_stubbed with AR associations queried via count: user.posts.count issues a SQL query; build_stubbed does not persist to DB, so count returns 0 or errors.
antipattern.reason: build_stubbed stubs AR persistence methods but not all query interfaces. For association assertions, allow(user).to receive(:posts).and_return([post]) or use create.

antipattern.description: travel_to without the block form: time freezes and never resets; subsequent specs run with stale frozen time.
antipattern.reason: Without travel_back or the block form, ActiveSupport::Testing::TimeHelpers leaves time pinned globally for the rest of the suite.

antipattern.description: Factory cascade bloat: create(:order) triggers user → profile → avatar creation; one spec inserts 5 records, slowing the suite by an order of magnitude.
antipattern.reason: Factory traits and associations compose automatically; agents copy the nearest factory call. Use build_stubbed or define traits without auto-associations.

antipattern.description: Shoulda-matchers on uncalled validators: validate_uniqueness_of(:email) passes even without a DB unique index, giving false confidence.
antipattern.reason: Shoulda-matchers check model validations in memory; they do not probe the DB constraint. Pair with a request spec that hits the actual constraint.
